subparts.text: Define "quality" and explain how it differs from "grade." 2.5
List and briefly describe any four dimensions of product quality. 2.5
Enlist the steps involved in conducting a benchmarking study. 2.5
Define FMEA? What is its purpose? 2.5
Differentiate between Histogram and Box Plot. When would you use each in Six Sigma? 2.5
A process has a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 5. If specification limits are 40 and 60, calculate the process capability index. 2.5
List any two control phase tools in Six Sigma and explain their purpose. 2.5
Explain Poka-Yoke? Give one real-life example of its application. 2.5

questions.question_text: Explain the role of quality statements in shaping an organization’s TQM efforts with suitable examples.
Describe the key features and benefits of adopting a customer- oriented approach in quality management. How is it different from a product-oriented approach?
Explain the concept of Taguchi’s robust design approach. How does it minimize quality loss? Illustrate with an example.
Differentiate between internal and competitive benchmarking. Which is more effective in driving quality improvement and why?
Explain various interrelationship diagrams in Six Sigma. Describe how they are constructed and interpreted.
Discuss how SIPOC diagrams help in defining the scope of a Six Sigma project. Why is it important to involve cross-functional teams in SIPOC creation?
Explain the process and importance of implementing Value Stream Mapping in Six Sigma. What insights can it provide for lean improvement?

A quality analyst is testing three machines to see if there is a significant difference in their mean output. The output in units from a sample run is as follows: Machine A Machine B Machine C 50 54 51 52 56 53 49 55 52 Conduct a One-Way ANOVA test at α = 0.05 and interpret the results.
